stories.title: Skubal Confirmed to Start Friday — Two-Time Cy Young Returns to Detroit Rotation
stories.angle: After a dominant June 7 rehab start (5 IP, 0 ER, 6K, 2H, 54 pitches at High-A West Michigan), Tarik Skubal is confirmed to start Friday June 12 at Cleveland. A.J. Hinch called his recovery "very encouraging" and the two-time Cy Young winner will return to a Tigers rotation that went 6-21 without him. Trade deadline watch intensifies: every contender monitoring the situation, with ESPN detailing nine blockbuster trade proposals.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal is back.

Two-time Cy Young. One dominant rehab start. Now targeting Friday at Cleveland.

The Tigers went 6-21 without him. Every contender is watching.

The Aug. 3 trade deadline just got a lot more interesting.

#Tigers #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Skubal. Friday. Cleveland.

The two-time Cy Young winner returns to the Detroit rotation this week — and every team at the trade deadline has to answer a simple question:

Do you go get him, or do you let someone else do it?

#Tigers #MLBTradeDeadline

article.headline: Skubal Confirmed to Start Friday — Two-Time Cy Young Returns to Detroit Rotation

research.facts:
Skubal Return Confirmed for Friday June 12
**Sources consulted:** Detroit News (June 8, 9), ClickOnDetroit/AJ Hinch quote (June 9), ESPN blockbuster trade proposals (June 2026), Yardbarker/TotalProSports (Skubal timeline)
**Key verified facts:**
- Skubal had arthroscopic left elbow surgery on May 6, 2026 [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Skubal made dominant rehab start June 7 with High-A West Michigan: 5 IP, 0 ER, 6K, 2H, 54 pitches [HIGH — Detroit News]
- Detroit News headline (June 9): "Detroit Tigers, Tarik Skubal ready to roll in Cleveland this weekend" — confirms target return weekend of June 12-13 [HIGH]
- A.J. Hinch said: "Very encouraged with Tarik. He looked exactly kind of how he's looked in the live BPs. The velocity, the execution was good." [HIGH — ClickOnDetroit]
- Tigers went 6-21 without Skubal [HIGH — consistent across multiple stories]
- Skubal is a two-time Cy Young Award winner [HIGH]
- ESPN had 9 blockbuster trade proposals involving Skubal — every contender watching [HIGH — ESPN piece confirmed]
- Skubal's trade deadline eligibility: he's a free agent after 2026 [HIGH — referenced in multiple stories]
**What I don't know:**
- Exact current ERA/stats from 2025 season or 2026 pre-injury stats — don't fabricate
- Whether the June 12 start is Game 1 of a Cleveland series or another game — "weekend" in Cleveland, likely Friday-Sunday

stories.title: Schwarber Leads MLB at 23 HRs — On Pace for 60-HR Club
stories.angle: Kyle Schwarber leads MLB with 23 home runs — one ahead of Yordan Alvarez (22). He hit his first 20 HRs through 49 team games, the earliest since Josh Hamilton in 2012. His pace projects to 60+ HR, which would make him the 5th member of the all-time 60-HR club (Bonds/McGwire/Sosa/Judge). Aaron Judge is on IL, Murakami also sidelined — Schwarber alone atop the leaderboard. Ben Rice third at 18.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber has 23 home runs.

Yordan Alvarez is second at 22. Judge is on the IL. Murakami is on the IL.

Schwarber is alone on top of the HR leaderboard — and on a pace that would make him just the 6th player in history to reach 60.

#Phillies #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
The 60-HR club: Bonds. McGwire. Sosa. Maris. Judge.

Five players. Ever.

Kyle Schwarber is on pace to join them in 2026.

He's got 23 HRs and 92 games left.

#Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Schwarber Leads MLB With 23 HRs and a 60-HR Pace — Chasing Baseball's Most Exclusive Club

research.facts:
Schwarber HR Race — 23 HRs, 60-HR Pace
**Sources consulted:** ESPN (Schwarber 60-HR pace piece), ClutchPoints (Schwarber 20th HR), Heavy.com (HR pace analysis), TSN repost of ESPN piece
**Key verified facts:**
- Schwarber leads MLB with 23 home runs as of June 10, 2026 [HIGH — CBS Sports stat leaders]
- Yordan Alvarez second at 22 HRs [HIGH — CBS Sports]
- Ben Rice third at 18 HRs [HIGH — CBS Sports]
- Aaron Judge on IL (rib stress fracture) — not in HR race currently [HIGH]
- Schwarber hit 20 HRs through first 49 team games [HIGH — ESPN piece]
- 20 HRs through 49 games is the fastest since Josh Hamilton in 2012 [HIGH — ESPN]
- Schwarber's pace at that point projected to 66 HRs [HIGH — ESPN projection at that time]
- 60-HR club members: Barry Bonds (73, 2001), Mark McGwire (70, 1998; 65, 1999), Sammy Sosa (66, 1998; 63, 1999; 64, 2001), Roger Maris (61, 1961), Aaron Judge (62, 2022) [HIGH — baseball history]
- Schwarber joining 60-HR club would make him 5th member (if counting distinct players, not seasons) [MEDIUM — need careful counting: Bonds, McGwire, Sosa, Maris, Judge = 5 have done it. Schwarber would be 6th player, not 5th — need care here]
**Careful note on 60-HR club:**
Players who have hit 60+ HRs in a season: Roger Maris (61 — 1961), Sammy Sosa (66 — 1998, 63 — 1999, 64 — 2001), Mark McGwire (70 — 1998, 65 — 1999), Barry Bonds (73 — 2001), Aaron Judge (62 — 2022). So 5 distinct players. Schwarber would be the 6th if he reaches 60. [HIGH — baseball reference]
**What I don't know:**
- Current actual game-by-game pace (updated June 10) — use "leading MLB with 23 HRs" and project from available data

stories.title: Yankees 7-5 in 10 Innings — Two Straight Without Judge, Bellinger Walks It Off
stories.angle: The Yankees defeated the Cleveland Guardians 7-5 in 10 innings Monday, their second straight win without Aaron Judge (rib stress fracture, IL). Cody Bellinger hit a walk-off 2-run single off Shawn Armstrong. Paul Goldschmidt and Ryan McMahon homered. Spencer Jones recalled from Triple-A. Yankees now 37-26 (or similar) and still in the AL East race with Ben Rice providing stability.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The Yankees are 2-0 without Aaron Judge.

Bellinger walked it off in the 10th Monday night. Goldschmidt and McMahon homered. 7-5 over Cleveland.

The AL East race is still very much alive.

#Yankees #MLB

article.headline: Yankees 7-5 in 10 Innings — Bellinger Walks It Off, New York Goes 2-0 Without Judge

research.facts:
Yankees 7-5 in 10 Innings
**Sources consulted:** Fox Sports game recap, Pinstripe Alley (June 9), ESPN box score

- Yankees defeated Guardians 7-5 in 10 innings [HIGH — Fox Sports, ESPN]
- This was the Yankees' second straight win without Judge [HIGH — Fox Sports headline]
- Cody Bellinger hit a walk-off 2-run single off Shawn Armstrong (1-1) in the 10th [HIGH — Pinstripe Alley/ESPN]
- Paul Goldschmidt and Ryan McMahon homered for the Yankees [HIGH — ESPN recap]
- Angel Martinez hit a 2-run HR in the 5th for Cleveland (5-4 lead) [HIGH — ESPN]

- Spencer Jones recalled from Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre [HIGH — prior research]

- Whether there's a June 10 game in this series — search showed it's a series

stories.title: Braves 45-21 — Most Dramatic Turnaround in Baseball
stories.angle: The Atlanta Braves are 45-21 as of early June — best record in baseball — under first-year manager Walt Weiss. Last season they were 76-86 and missed the playoffs. That's a 24-game improvement in the win column pace. They lead the NL East by a wide margin. The story behind the turnaround: Chris Sale anchoring the rotation, Ronald Acuña back in the lineup, and the front office rebuilding the roster.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The Atlanta Braves are 45-21.

Best record in baseball.

Last year they were 76-86.

That's not a turnaround. That's a transformation.

#Braves #MLB

article.headline: The Braves Are 45-21 — And Baseball's Most Dramatic Turnaround Story

stories.title: June Milestones — Sánchez's Scoreless Streak, Tigers' Walk-Off Record, Reds' Clutch Anomaly
stories.angle: Three unusual milestones from June 2026: (1) Cristopher Sánchez (Phillies) extended his scoreless streak to 50.2 innings as of June 9, breaking a 115-year Phillies franchise record set by Grover Cleveland Alexander. (2) The 2026 Detroit Tigers have allowed 4 walk-off HRs when leading — already tying the single-season record (1985 Twins, 2006 Indians). (3) The Cincinnati Reds are the 4th team in MLB history to win their first 10 one-run games (.203 BA — extreme statistical anomaly).

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
June milestones that make you look twice:

→ Sánchez: 50+ scoreless IP, breaks 115-year Phillies record
→ Tigers: 4 walk-offs allowed while leading — ties MLB single-season record
→ Reds: 10-0 in one-run games with a .203 BA

Baseball never gets old. #MLB

article.headline: June's Strangest MLB Stories — Sánchez's Historic Streak, Detroit's Walk-Off Nightmare, and the Reds' Anomaly

research.facts:
June Milestones
**Sources consulted:** Fox Sports MLB milestones tracker (2026), MLB.com "stats defining 2026 season"
**Key verified facts:**
- Cristopher Sánchez (Phillies) broke a 115-year Phillies franchise record for scoreless innings — the previous record was held by Grover Cleveland Alexander [HIGH — Fox Sports milestones tracker]
- Sánchez's scoreless streak reached 50.2 innings (as of June 9, per prior pipeline research) [HIGH — prior research June 9]
- Detroit Tigers have allowed 4 walk-off HRs when leading in 2026 — ties single-season record (1985 Twins, 2006 Cleveland Indians) [HIGH — Fox Sports milestones tracker / MLB.com]
- Cincinnati Reds are the 4th team in MLB history to win all of their first 10 one-run/two-run games [HIGH — MLB.com stats]
- Reds have a .203 team batting average in this context — they're winning close games despite poor overall hitting [HIGH — MLB.com]
- José Ramírez reached a milestone per the milestones tracker [MEDIUM — mentioned but details unclear from search summary, don't fabricate]
**What I don't know:**
- Exact Sánchez streak total as of June 10 (streak may have continued or ended since June 9)
- Ramirez's specific milestone — don't use without confirmation
